ranked_movies.title: The Thing
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as a top-tier horror film with intense practical effects and a gripping story of paranoia and isolation. Multiple users across different threads mentioned it as a classic and a personal favorite.

ranked_movies.title: Sinister
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted for its effective use of horror elements that genuinely scare viewers, with specific mention of its soundtrack and the concept of the 'super 8 films' being particularly frightening.

ranked_movies.title: The Blair Witch Project
ranked_movies.reason: Brought up repeatedly as a film that provides an immersive and realistic horror experience, especially for those who saw it in theaters during its initial release. It's noted for its unique found-footage style that has influenced many later films.

ranked_movies.title: Hereditary
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a modern horror masterpiece that deeply affects viewers with its intense and emotional narrative, particularly the climax which left a strong impression on many.

ranked_movies.title: The Ring
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a film that terrified a generation, especially with its use of technology (like the cursed video) as a horror element. Its impact on the horror genre and viewers' psyche is frequently discussed.

ranked_movies.title: The Exorcist
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently mentioned as one of the scariest films ever made, even after decades. Its impact on horror, especially in terms of religious horror, is undisputed.

ranked_movies.title: 28 Days Later
ranked_movies.reason: Credited with revitalizing the zombie genre and introducing the faster, more aggressive infected beings that are now a staple in horror.

ranked_movies.title: The Witch
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for its atmospheric dread and historical authenticity. It's often cited as a film that uses slow burn horror effectively, leaving a lasting impression.

ranked_movies.title: It Follows
ranked_movies.reason: Praised for its unique premise of an unstoppable, slowly approaching entity. The film's technique of hiding the entity in plain sight in wide shots is particularly noted as innovative and terrifying.

ranked_movies.title: The Wailing
ranked_movies.reason: A foreign horror film (Korean) that received high praise for its complexity and ability to combine multiple horror elements effectively. It's often mentioned as a hidden gem that deserves more attention.

ranked_movies.title: Event Horizon
ranked_movies.reason: Described as a sci-fi horror hybrid that has a strong cult following. It's frequently mentioned in discussions about space horror and films that have a strong visual style.

ranked_movies.title: The Descent
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for its intense claustrophobic atmosphere and as a film that effectively uses its setting to enhance horror. It's often brought up in discussions about horror that relies on physical space and isolation.

ranked_movies.title: The Autopsy of Jane Doe
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a film that builds dread effectively and has a strong payoff. It's often recommended as a modern horror that does a lot with a little.

ranked_movies.title: The Conjuring
ranked_movies.reason: As part of a popular franchise, it's frequently mentioned as a well-made horror film that balances jump scares with story. It's also noted as a gateway horror film for newcomers to the genre.

ranked_movies.title: The Others
ranked_movies.reason: Praised for its story and twist ending. It's often mentioned as a horror film that also works as a drama, making it accessible to non-horror fans.

ranked_movies.title: The Texas Chainsaw Massacre (1974)
ranked_movies.reason: Often cited as one of the most intense and raw horror experiences, especially for its time. It's a benchmark for horror that relies on atmosphere and suggestion over gore.

ranked_movies.title: Poltergeist
ranked_movies.reason: A classic that's often remembered for being both a family film and a horror film. It's mentioned as a film that introduced many to horror in a way that wasn't overly traumatic.

ranked_movies.title: The Babadook
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for its psychological depth and the way it uses horror to explore grief. It's often brought up in discussions about horror that is also art.

ranked_movies.title: The Ring
ranked_movies.reason: As above, but also noted for its international impact, having been remade and still being discussed in terms of its imagery and the fear of technology.

ranked_movies.title: The Blair Witch Project
ranked_movies.reason: As above, but also noted for its marketing and the way it used the internet to create hype, a precursor to modern viral marketing in horror.
